retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Traditional technique complications
retrieved_docs.content: Traditional methods for anterior access cavity preparation carry a range of potential complications when executed without sufficient precision. An incomplete convenience extension may produce a pear-shaped apical canal, limiting effective shaping and cleaning. [60] [61] [62] Retention of the lingual shoulder and cavity margin can cause the instrument shaft to rest against these structures, preventing thorough obturation and debridement and ultimately predisposing the case to failure. [40] [61] [62]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Guided Access Cavity
retrieved_docs.content: Guided endodontics relies on CBCT combined with a surface scan of the tooth to plan access. Static navigation uses this data to produce a 3D-printed template with an integrated sleeve, directing the bur along a predetermined trajectory. [68] [73] [74] Dynamic navigation, by contrast, employs a marker–camera–computer system that tracks the instrument in real time, allowing continuous visualization of its position throughout the procedure. [75] [72] Both approaches improve accuracy, reduce the risk of iatrogenic errors, and facilitate minimally invasive access, particularly in calcified or anatomically complex anterior teeth. Studies examining static protocols have demonstrated the clinical feasibility of template-guided access, while dynamic systems have expanded the flexibility and adaptability of guided endodontic techniques in contemporary practice. [68] [73] [74]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Traditional Methods for Anterior Access Cavity Creation
retrieved_docs.content: With the lingual shoulder cleared and incisal access established, instruments can approach the apical third of the canal without restriction. The prepared cavity reflects the shape of the canal system, supporting round, tapered filling materials placed into the apical third. Circumferential filling instruments or Gates-Glidden drills are then employed to clean and shape the remaining ovoid portion of the canal, ensuring adequate debridement and proper preparation for obturation. [38] [56] [57]

retrieved_docs.title: Oral Surgery, Extraction of Roots -- Technique or Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: Endodontic File Technique: Using endodontic files to retrieve retained root segments involves the insertion of an endodontic file through the visible root canal orifice from the coronal aspect and advancing it apically until friction develops within the canal. [23] The more contact surface area the file has within the canal, the more generated force when pulling the root segment coronally. [23] The type and size of endodontic file best suited for extraction in root tips have yet to be well-studied, but it has been shown that a #25 Hedstrom file generates a tremendous pullout force in all roots, regardless of the length of the root. Factors such as root length and PDL attachment significantly impact the use of this technique. Shorter root segments and tips offer the greatest likelihood of technique success. Barbed broaches, size #40 or #50 endodontic reamers, and even file sizes up to #80 were all identified as potential adjuncts. [23] [24] However, larger file sizes may restrict the distance of apical advancement of the file within the canal, limiting the amount of frictional force. Also, due to the significant amount of friction generated within the canal, imprudent amounts of rotational force can cause files to break, further complicating the extraction. [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Apical Periodontitis -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: However, some periapical lesions may persist despite following the most strict and careful clinical protocol due to the complex anatomy of the root canal system. [17] In cases of post-treatment disease, non-surgical endodontic retreatment or periradicular surgery are treatment alternatives to save the tooth. [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Apical Periodontitis -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: A periapical radiolucency often persists when the root canal treatment cannot control the infection because some clinical steps were not adequately followed, such as insufficient aseptic control, poor instrumentation, inadequate access cavity design, unreached canals, and restoration leakage. [17]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Traditional Methods for Anterior Access Cavity Creation
retrieved_docs.content: In contrast, "adult" teeth exhibit ovoid pulp chambers partially filled with secondary dentin. To enable the bur shaft and instruments to function in the central axis, convenience extension must be advanced further initially when the radiograph shows advanced pulpal recession. The preparation funnels down toward the canal orifice, which becomes increasingly difficult to reach with a round bur as the pulp recedes, often necessitating a deeper initial extension. [56] [59] [57] Such an extension ensures that the bur shaft and instruments remain aligned with the central axis, even in cases with a reduced pulp space. [56] [59] [57]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Truss Access Cavity
retrieved_docs.content: The TrussAC method is generally applied in multiple-rooted teeth to maintain the dentinal bridge between 2 or more small cavities that have been prepared to access the canal orifices in each root. [7] [43] [69]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Filling root canals, commonly called obturation, is a critical step in successful treatment. The goals of obturation are twofold: to create an apical seal and fill the root canal without incorporating voids within the filling material. [1] [2] Successful obturation eliminates leakage of contaminants into the channel, such as saliva, bacteria, and periapical tissue fluid, and entraps any residual microorganisms within the canal space. [2] The ability to adequately obturate a root canal is directly related to the preceding step of cleaning and shaping. Cleaning and shaping refer to a root canal's mechanical and chemical preparation to remove organic and inorganic matter. A canal that has been poorly cleaned and shaped results in equally poor obturation and is one of the strongest indicators of treatment failure. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Advantages and Disadvantages
retrieved_docs.content: Guided access techniques Utilizes 3D imaging and computer-guided templates to create highly precise, minimally invasive access cavities. Advantages: High accuracy, conserves tooth structure, reduces iatrogenic errors. Disadvantages: Requires specialized equipment, is costly, and requires a significant amount of planning time. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Traditional technique complications
retrieved_docs.content: Craniocervical perforation may develop when the convenience extension is left unfinished before the bur shaft enters the canal space. [63] [61] [62] Pulp debris left behind during preparation may also discolor the crown, especially when the access cavity lacks proper incisal extension and lies too close to the gingival margin. [63] [62] [40] Misjudgment of the tooth's lingual-axial or mesial-axial inclination can result in abrasion of the labial or distal wall during instrumentation. [63] [61] [62] Failure to complete the convenience extension can create a ledge at the apical-labial curve, where the instrument shaft rests on the shoulder or cavity edge instead of advancing smoothly into the canal. [63] [61] [64]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Root canals are 3D structures with complex and unique anatomy; no 2 are alike. Achieving a 3D obturation with complete coronal, lateral, and apical seals is essential for long-term success. [1] Radiographs are still the main method used to visualize root canals during the diagnostic and treatment stages. However, one of their major weaknesses is that they only provide a 2D representation of 3D canals. [3] As a result, it is impossible to assess the quality of the canal seal created during the obturation stage. Introducing micro-CT imaging into the field of endodontics has dramatically enhanced the accuracy of diagnosis, cleaning shaping, and, ultimately, obturation. [4] There are countless materials and techniques for obturation. Most techniques employ a sealer and a core filling material to ensure complete obturation. This activity discusses available sealers and proceeds to core obturation materials.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Properties -- Gutta-Percha
retrieved_docs.content: Gutta-percha has many advantages, such as ease of manipulation, radiopacity, low toxicity, and plasticity. However, it does not bond to dentin and shrinks when cooled. [5] [25] Gutta-percha cones are available in standardized 0.02 taper as specified by the International Organization of Standardization and non-standardized sizes. [5] This allows clinicians to mechanically prepare the canal space using various commercial rotary systems using standardized file sizes. When the canal is prepared to a given file size, a gutta-percha cone of the same size can be inserted and adapted to the canal. [1] [5] [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Review Questions
retrieved_docs.content: Access free multiple choice questions on this topic. Comment on this article.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Optimal endodontic care for anterior teeth requires a precise understanding of pulp chamber anatomy, careful selection of access cavity design, and minimally invasive techniques to preserve tooth structure while ensuring thorough canal debridement. Traditional, conservative, ultraconservative, truss, and guided access approaches each offer advantages depending on tooth morphology, pulp status, and procedural complexity. Advances in imaging, guided navigation, and restorative technologies have enhanced the precision and safety of anterior endodontic procedures, reducing iatrogenic errors and improving long-term outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Tooth -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Lateral canals can be present in different numbers in a certain tooth and cause problems during endodontic or root therapies. Radiopaque materials can be used to indicate the number and position of these canals.

This relationship underscores the importance of the cervical canal in the context of procedures targeting female organ systems.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2294
retrieved_docs.content: Combined obstetrical version is a broad category of obstetric procedures that encompasses the specific technique known as internal and combined version without extraction. The internal and combined version without extraction is identified as a subtype of combined obstetrical version and is classified under the broader group of other delivery procedures, indicating its role as an alternative method used in the management of childbirth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2071
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy-Related Procedures comprise a range of interventions used to manage complex obstetric conditions and facilitate safe delivery. Among these, the surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancy and abdominal pregnancy represents critical operative approaches for addressing abnormal implantation of the embryo, thereby preventing life‑threatening complications for the mother. In addition, Vaginal Assisted Delivery is included as a procedural option that supports the progression of labor and assists in the birth of the infant when additional support is required. Together, these child procedures under the umbrella of Pregnancy-Related Procedures illustrate the spectrum of surgical and assisted techniques employed in modern obstetric care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2912
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy-Related Procedures is a broad category of obstetric interventions that encompasses specific techniques such as بضع الفرج and Vaginal Assisted Delivery, each classified as a child procedure within this group. Both بضع الفرج and Vaginal Assisted Delivery represent assisted vaginal delivery methods used to facilitate safe childbirth when spontaneous vaginal delivery is not optimal, highlighting the importance of tailored procedural options in maternal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 1 -- Community 81
retrieved_docs.content: surgical approach. The shared clinical goal across all listed procedures is the restoration of normal anatomy and function by closing abnormal fistulous tracts, thereby guiding clinicians in selecting the optimal operative technique based on fistula location, morphology, and required reconstruction.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2791
retrieved_docs.content: Episiotomy and تمزق الأغشية الاصطناعي (artificial membrane rupture) are both classified under الإجراءات العلاجية الخاصة بالتوليد (obstetric therapeutic procedures). By recognizing these interventions as subcomponents of the broader category of obstetric therapeutic care, clinicians can better organize treatment options, understand their related indications, and apply appropriate management strategies within the field of obstetrics.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 3 -- Community 1752
retrieved_docs.content: Complete excision is a broad surgical principle that entails the total removal of targeted tissue, and the Arabic term استئصال العقد اللمفاوية الجذري represents a specific application of this principle in which the root lymph nodes are entirely removed. This relationship highlights that radical lymph node excision (استئصال العقد اللمفاوية الجذري) is a particular form of complete excision, illustrating how general surgical concepts are operationalized in specialized procedures.

retrieved_docs.content: of menstruation”) are localized to these organs, with infertility positioned within female reproductive function. The infectious process is identified as the underlying mechanism of post‑abortion complications, linking infection directly to adverse outcomes after termination procedures. Collectively, this structured framework interrelates prenatal surveillance, invasive diagnostics, therapeutic interventions, delivery assistance, placental and ectopic‑pregnancy management, and post‑delivery care, providing a comprehensive reference for clinical documentation, education, and patient‑centered obstetric management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 2076
retrieved_docs.content: Complete excision is a broad surgical concept of removing tissue entirely, and استئصال العقد اللمفاوية الجذري (root lymph‑node excision) is a specific type of complete excision. استئصال العقد اللمفاوية الجذري is also listed as a member of the مجموعة الإجراءات العلاجية والتشخيصية ذات الصلة بالأمراض الخبيثة (SMQ), indicating that this root lymph‑node excision procedure is classified among the therapeutic and diagnostic interventions used for malignant diseases. This relationship clarifies that performing a complete excision of the root lymph nodes is both a distinct surgical technique and an integral part of the larger set of procedures aimed at diagnosing and treating cancer.
